Friend,
---
So many kind souls like you have asked how I’m doing, so I wanted to personally update you on my recovery.
---
I was hospitalized after a devastating car crash. We were struck by a vehicle going what felt like 70-80 miles per hour. Had I not been wearing a seatbelt, I would’ve been killed.
---
Right now, a back brace is holding my fractured spine together.**
---
But if I could survive being arrested in the most violent jail in America, I know I can survive **__ANYTHING!__**
---
[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=4beeaff5d51f4325aca3245826d80eaf&_e=6JhZk_YbRvV_HlnEGPSbUo9izoxvWGVI2cCeG-66dYFQt2bka0iI8awDD1R024yTr81Syw9QxbC5_vFaEmVutVpqs_D3GWnAAET64nnZT3mfvozgIkzsliuA3YqkAozwFVLJfy9kBgVNQb3SlXMnKCPlQvr5IUfTdy8gcYc6x49x7qmTnmYpSRZy4z1h-3cdt2DiOamDSoLAWGuwDPsT4WTGWD-87AuPAcsQYQDiY30VK2jMyUMGJKkdsW0e0i4-DOsnoN87AY8qK6ToevXPXhp4UPbCG632XiZaxRQat0xH0VF4GVj6f_gU0cnkkUwCXSm0ON8jHqf0_M_zoINjlC6kYDjmlUUyqClkFXl_-EEGoA05UAfPLUolu1p81-IJiEZRX8XU4JqR9pV0QB3CL5FIPruuUO3yPxJGX50gzuq6jU8EJ0Ndq2rHziZu-PdKbbYd5QBcHtVW9W_aBJCui7gTMCBjOCBCBkh-zWUsSBFZMXWjl5HT0rymKFC7TJZ-dSt0Tx9Qg2W8t9BsWFHnOwEgto5cew6iojr-OQ35oKTPE8hLM_17HITrjDKu7zpvt_HdrJqduGvbv6Cnt4Ym706zu3QS8_CRRFN99bf_rDvQ-FdDjI5dA3WW7cndtItqhRsE9uMAqXhy67osmRe8fQ%3D%3D)
__
---
One thing especially helped take the pain away...
---
...**President Trump has announced that he will award me with the Presidential Medal of Freedom – _the highest civilian honor in America._**
---
**President Trump knows I’m in a dire situation right now. His pardon powers only apply to _federal_ cases, which means he _legally_ _cannot_ save me from the bogus _state-level_ charges brought against me by a radical Democrat Attorney General who is trying to imprison me for 20 YEARS.**
---
**Left with no pardon, no safety net, and no lifeline, my _ONLY_ chance at walking free is to secure a top-notch defense team.**
---
But the reality is, the Left had already forced me to the point of **BANKRUPTCY**.
---
**And now, this car crash has set me back even more...**
---
And so, my last remaining hope of assembling the quality of defense required to BEAT the Left’s sham charges and WIN the trial of my life is patriots like _YOU_.
---
That’s why I’m making a final plea to humbly ask that you contribute whatever amount you can to my Legal Defense Trust as I continue fighting for my _FREEDOM_ and my _JUSTICE_.
---
__Because when President Trump places the Medal of Freedom around my neck, I want history to always remember that I FOUGHT for my freedom and justice until the VERY END!__
---
[**So, if you can, please make a contribution to the Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund to ensure I have the resources needed to retain a top-notch defense team in what is now the FINAL BATTLE for my freedom and justice.**](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=4beeaff5d51f4325aca3245826d80eaf&_e=6JhZk_YbRvV_HlnEGPSbUo9izoxvWGVI2cCeG-66dYFQt2bka0iI8awDD1R024yTr81Syw9QxbC5_vFaEmVutVpqs_D3GWnAAET64nnZT3mfvozgIkzsliuA3YqkAozwFVLJfy9kBgVNQb3SlXMnKCPlQvr5IUfTdy8gcYc6x49x7qmTnmYpSRZy4z1h-3cdt2DiOamDSoLAWGuwDPsT4e86LHcbZ8Tj2g3OzTnlfAOLx4-Yv_jtJM4f2opQMWObdao4ivWWJuIPJV6yc0J9Z7-8vA9Ohxksv1nXQaGGR1c-gRHZec46sRQmm09MKGeODtz_E1sjBvm8B5ttYmnUfcXLya2eDkDJaryW5x_sFdNsRLjygCmTB_Zmz1Z4Hhb8lUZgCbGU1bJBbPcIHH9veKaGoo70bLj7apVsagh0JqUWbVj5nmDon432PS-IZbN6BK2cJnHj4MvhfHMHUqxM3Y05QW_ghOhwc--4L85N6XG-s1FhkRoU9Yai4nhwKMKJWpSDSDMN_TlvA_8gxpcdtye8d6ehpdNMSxuJlX5fimYWULX3VMSLfpMzkfyNvmhlI0HPPB3yp4ZSLJJQhRHMlt798RiUck18uN27MtSk1ZXNfjX-TnvmeElEGibvLbOnUosSRYThtmbSnaimGmjYcQ%3D%3D)

clean_body: Because you’ve been a loyal supporter, I wanted you to hear this directly from me. | Friend, So many kind souls like you have asked how I’m doing, so I wanted to personally update you on my recovery. I was hospitalized after a devastating car crash. We were struck by a vehicle going what felt like 70-80 miles per hour. Had I not been wearing a seatbelt, I would’ve been killed. Right now, a back brace is holding my fractured spine together. But if I could survive being arrested in the most violent jail in America, I know I can survive ANYTHING! One thing especially helped take the pain away... ...President Trump has announced that he will award me with the Presidential Medal of Freedom – the highest civilian honor in America. President Trump knows I’m in a dire situation right now. His pardon powers only apply to federal cases, which means he legally cannot save me from the bogus state-level charges brought against me by a radical Democrat Attorney General who is trying to imprison me for 20 YEARS. Left with no pardon, no safety net, and no lifeline, my ONLY chance at walking free is to secure a top-notch defense team. But the reality is, the Left had already forced me to the point of BANKRUPTCY. And now, this car crash has set me back even more...** And so, my last remaining hope of assembling the quality of defense required to BEAT the Left’s sham charges and WIN the trial of my life is patriots like YOU. That’s why I’m making a final plea to humbly ask that you contribute whatever amount you can to my Legal Defense Trust as I continue fighting for my FREEDOM and my JUSTICE. Because when President Trump places the Medal of Freedom around my neck, I want history to always remember that I FOUGHT for my freedom and justice until the VERY END! Thank you and God bless you, Rudy Giuliani Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust

body:

# Start your Monday by [voting now](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=6efc93f338&e=b749ec4ed8) for the National Toddler of the Year!
---
[|
---](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=a409525ab6&e=b749ec4ed8)
| | Who will win the National title of Toddler of the Year and star in a national toy ad? **You** get to decide! You can [**vote for FREE**](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=8440920b4f&e=b749ec4ed8) once every day. [Click here now to cast your vote!](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=410576538f&e=b749ec4ed8)[ ](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=6aa87d5b94&e=b749ec4ed8)The **Quarterfinals end on Thursday September 11th** so make sure to [cast your **FREE vote**](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=7332c59913&e=b749ec4ed8) every single day from now until then.
ONLY the toddler who is ranked in 1st place on Thursday Sept. 11th will advance to the SemiFinals & can then move on to the Finals. Click [here](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=bd67eac856&e=b749ec4ed8) to add a [daily reminder](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=9dd0a38c9d&e=b749ec4ed8) to your calendar as an easy reminder to [vote](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=a702e2836d&e=b749ec4ed8) every day.
And if you want to cast extra votes, every [**$1 you donate = 1 extra vote**](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=b0094b85bf&e=b749ec4ed8) with **all proceeds supporting Toys for Tots**. Which means **your**[**donation**](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=b49dec9f16&e=b749ec4ed8)**is tax-deductible** , helps decide which kid wins the crown, AND supports toys going to kids in need this holiday season in cooperation with the US Marines. [](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=46ddc6c8a1&e=b749ec4ed8)
**Start your Monday with by casting your**[**FREE VOTE NOW**](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=3c89cd85d8&e=b749ec4ed8)**.** We need everyone to make sure to [vote for Sophia](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=66becfe246&e=b749ec4ed8) every day from now until Thursday. You can [cast one FREE vote every 24 hours](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=1f3e4e0a11&e=b749ec4ed8), or make a **TAX DEDUCTIBLE DONATION** with every $1 donated = 1 extra vote all for the benefit of Toy for Tots.
To make a tax deductible donation by [giving $10](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=441768600d&e=b749ec4ed8) click [here](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=6a092e056c&e=b749ec4ed8). To make a tax deductible donation by [giving $25](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=8dd0d42e3f&e=b749ec4ed8) click [here](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=35ecbcbc4e&e=b749ec4ed8).To make a tax deductible donation by [giving $50](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=174b668a64&e=b749ec4ed8) click [here](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=5337099f8a&e=b749ec4ed8).To make a tax deductible donation by [giving $100](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=50072be700&e=b749ec4ed8) click [here](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=72090158c4&e=b749ec4ed8). To make a tax deductible donation by [giving $250](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=555effcd6a&e=b749ec4ed8) click [here](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=f809a8d965&e=b749ec4ed8).**ALL donations are TAX DEDUCTIBLE** & go to benefit Toys for Tots.
To cast a [FREE vote](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=47ca26eb66&e=b749ec4ed8) click [here](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=77175e6c7b&e=b749ec4ed8).
Thank you for helping [select ](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=e295946f6d&e=b749ec4ed8)the National Toddler of the Year & [supporting Toys for Tots](https://toddleroftheyear.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b753463ba6b7f88fcc279ec77&id=22c44be2b0&e=b749ec4ed8) in conjunction with the US Marines. Remember to vote DAILY from now until this round ends on Thursday evening Sept. 11th.

subject: The Battle Within
body:

# [The Battle Within](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=173021203&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMDIxMjAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTczMzMyMjMsImV4cCI6MTc1OTkyNTIyMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.RmPStCe-4KElBHpIw3UyRfbQthf22UVLSo0khx0okY4)
| | [Andrew Yang](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---
| Sep 8
---
| [](https://substack.com/@andrewyang)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=173021203&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMDIxMjAzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzMzMzIyMywiZXhwIjoxNzU5OTI1MjIzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjg3MzA0Iiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.Cs_S498oiZ6Xsq7SeSP_J6ZCF8cK42-myFysnM6Dzi8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=173021203&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMDIxMjAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTczMzMyMjMsImV4cCI6MTc1OTkyNTIyMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.RmPStCe-4KElBHpIw3UyRfbQthf22UVLSo0khx0okY4&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=287304&post_id=173021203&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMDIxMjAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTczMzMyMjMsImV4cCI6MTc1OTkyNTIyMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI4NzMwNCIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.RmPStCe-4KElBHpIw3UyRfbQthf22UVLSo0khx0okY4)
---
| | [](https://substack..Wu3RZeKwX1cfCB0c4Y2XJyxfBheUn_p3DHVnSwJSOHs?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/andrewyang/p/the-battle-within?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Hello, I hope the Fall is off to a great start for you and yours.
It's not for everyone. "Hey, my wife and I are getting a divorce." I got that phone call last week from a friend.
I was filled with sadness. They have a child who is friends with one of our boys. I have seen multiple friends go through a divorce and it's always very difficult on everyone.
"I'm so sorry to hear that. Please let me know if there's anything we can do to be supportive, including having [your child] over for a while," I said. "And thanks for letting us know."
When I was young and stupid, I used to think that a divorce said something about those involved. Over the years I figured out that wasn't true; it's more like a natural disaster that some people go through. As a friend put it, "There's nothing wrong with divorce, but there's a lot of value that gets destroyed, particularly if kids are involved." Among the friends I have whose marriages have ended, money was often a factor, as it is in most relationships, but generally not the main one. There was more often a disintegration of the relationship that was bigger than money struggles. Oftentimes, one partner became consumed with something, whether it was work or just becoming unhappy. In some cases, one partner seemed to experience mental or emotional problems.
I sometimes joke to people, "Still married!" when they ask how I'm doing. I'm grateful for that given that I spent the better part of several years on the road campaigning. Thank you to Evelyn for putting up with a lot! My parents were immigrants to the U.S. and are still together today; they seemed more occupied with trying to make their way in a new country than whether they were enjoying themselves.
One presumption you have is that other families are clicking on all cylinders, and your problems or your kids' problems are unique. But then you have a late night conversation with another parent and you hear what they're going through and think, "Wow, maybe I don't have it so bad." As Tolstoy put it, "All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way." Yes, I read that book a million years ago.
As a parent, you tend to focus on the external with your kids: what kind of neighborhood or school they're in, what kinds of activities to put them in, their nutrition and safety, that kind of thing. But occasionally they struggle and you feel totally powerless, because the struggle is nothing you can help with; it's happening inside of them. There's a meltdown, and you have a range of mitigation or damage control behaviors to choose from. Sometimes, you don't make the right choice because it isn't clear what that is. Every parent just hopes to make more decent choices than bad ones.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3eac251d-2be4-421b-8bce-822a8cd16fd0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
One of the themes we had at Venture for America was having something called an 'internal locus of control;' that is, you should act like you can control or impact the situation rather than think that it's happening to you. One attitude could be, "Oh man, that teacher doesn't like me." The internal question would be, "What could I do to improve this teacher's opinion of me?" You imagine that you can impact things positively. It's very useful for entrepreneurship.
It's useful for relationships too. What can you do to make the situation better? Some relationships are obviously beyond help, but others might not be. Often, we imagine that we can control or influence others, but the truth is that the person we have the most control over is ourselves. You start with self-control and go on from there. Sometimes, your self-control might rub off on someone.
The big begins with the small. I'm a serial entrepreneur who has designs on improving the human condition. But day-to-day the best thing I could probably do is wake up early, get some exercise, do something positive with or for Evelyn and the boys, check in with my Mom, try and be a solid friend to those who are going through struggles, and be as grateful as I can be for the good things and people in my life. And remember that it's okay to seek joy in things, even in difficult times.
If you're young or unattached, keep putting yourself in position to build relationships. It will pay off in the end, even if some of the experiences hurt along the way. There's a person on the other side.
Everyone has a fight they're waging, either external or internal. You sometimes can't control your circumstances, but you can determine your own actions and attitude. If you work on yourself, you'll have a much better shot at changing your tiny corner of the world. And who knows, it could grow a lot from there.
To see what Forward is doing in your area, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/9dd0175a-cda1-47a2-a68f-c83a7cd70413?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). For my interview of independent Nebraska Senate candidate Dan Osborn, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/624c8892-9f46-4601-8c44-266ca3f30a74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). I'm working on something that will get more buying power into people's hands that I'm very excited about. The next [Offline Party](https://substack.com/redirect/d4bc96f7-b1c3-454a-a3f1-11db3fa115db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is in New York on September 18th that will double as a launch party. Try and be present and grateful.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1eb6acff-e889-43a0-93de-9c663adcfc5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

clean_body: Hello, I hope the Fall is off to a great start for you and yours. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | ---|---|--- * | | | Sep 8 | | | | | | | | | | | | Hello, I hope the Fall is off to a great start for you and yours. It's not for everyone. "Hey, my wife and I are getting a divorce." I got that phone call last week from a friend. I was filled with sadness. They have a child who is friends with one of our boys. I have seen multiple friends go through a divorce and it's always very difficult on everyone. "I'm so sorry to hear that. Please let me know if there's anything we can do to be supportive, including having [your child] over for a while," I said. "And thanks for letting us know." When I was young and stupid, I used to think that a divorce said something about those involved. Over the years I figured out that wasn't true; it's more like a natural disaster that some people go through. As a friend put it, "There's nothing wrong with divorce, but there's a lot of value that gets destroyed, particularly if kids are involved." Among the friends I have whose marriages have ended, money was often a factor, as it is in most relationships, but generally not the main one. There was more often a disintegration of the relationship that was bigger than money struggles. Oftentimes, one partner became consumed with something, whether it was work or just becoming unhappy. In some cases, one partner seemed to experience mental or emotional problems. I sometimes joke to people, "Still married!" when they ask how I'm doing. I'm grateful for that given that I spent the better part of several years on the road campaigning. Thank you to Evelyn for putting up with a lot! My parents were immigrants to the U.S. and are still together today; they seemed more occupied with trying to make their way in a new country than whether they were enjoying themselves. One presumption you have is that other families are clicking on all cylinders, and your problems or your kids' problems are unique. But then you have a late night conversation with another parent and you hear what they're going through and think, "Wow, maybe I don't have it so bad." As Tolstoy put it, "All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way." Yes, I read that book a million years ago. As a parent, you tend to focus on the external with your kids: what kind of neighborhood or school they're in, what kinds of activities to put them in, their nutrition and safety, that kind of thing. But occasionally they struggle and you feel totally powerless, because the struggle is nothing you can help with; it's happening inside of them. There's a meltdown, and you have a range of mitigation or damage control behaviors to choose from. Sometimes, you don't make the right choice because it isn't clear what that is. Every parent just hopes to make more decent choices than bad ones. | | ---|---|--- One of the themes we had at Venture for America was having something called an 'internal locus of control;' that is, you should act like you can control or impact the situation rather than think that it's happening to you. One attitude could be, "Oh man, that teacher doesn't like me." The internal question would be, "What could I do to improve this teacher's opinion of me?" You imagine that you can impact things positively. It's very useful for entrepreneurship. It's useful for relationships too. What can you do to make the situation better? Some relationships are obviously beyond help, but others might not be. Often, we imagine that we can control or influence others, but the truth is that the person we have the most control over is ourselves. You start with self-control and go on from there. Sometimes, your self-control might rub off on someone. The big begins with the small. I'm a serial entrepreneur who has designs on improving the human condition. But day-to-day the best thing I could probably do is wake up early, get some exercise, do something positive with or for Evelyn and the boys, check in with my Mom, try and be a solid friend to those who are going through struggles, and be as grateful as I can be for the good things and people in my life. And remember that it's okay to seek joy in things, even in difficult times. If you're young or unattached, keep putting yourself in position to build relationships. It will pay off in the end, even if some of the experiences hurt along the way. There's a person on the other side. Everyone has a fight they're waging, either external or internal. You sometimes can't control your circumstances, but you can determine your own actions and attitude. If you work on yourself, you'll have a much better shot at changing your tiny corner of the world. And who knows, it could grow a lot from there. To see what Forward is doing in your area, click . For my interview of independent Nebraska Senate candidate Dan Osborn, click . I'm working on something that will get more buying power into people's hands that I'm very excited about. The next is in New York on September 18th that will double as a launch party. Try and be present and grateful. | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Andrew Yang 111 Sutter Street, 7th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

# [The Myth of the Perfect Candidate](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5156566&post_id=171417718&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcxNDE3NzE4LCJpYXQiOjE3NTczMzQwMjUsImV4cCI6MTc1OTkyNjAyNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTUxNTY1NjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2OYJk_B84XC00TuYYI1vnM_nzue4lBVCxhGj2Aq6nR0)
### We're chasing ghosts with impossible litmus tests
| | [Matt Royer](https://substack.com/@bytheballot)

Imagine your perfect political candidate. What's their background? Where do they come from? What do they look like? What is their belief system? Are they religious? Do they have a family? What's their sexual orientation? What's their gender identity? Where do they stand on current issues? National issues? International issues?
Do you have them? Good.
Chances are slim that any two readers will imagine the same candidate, given the infinite possible combinations.
Unfortunately, this is also how many Americans approach the ballot box. Every individual voter is entitled to their own litmus test to determine whether or not they want to vote for someone. But when a candidate checks all but one of your boxes and you walk away, it creates an impossible environment where candidates must have an opinion on every niche issue--or risk losing votes or, worse, facing a digital dogpile for dismissing a potential voter.
Living in a democracy is both a blessing and a curse when it comes to political discourse. The meeting and discussion of ideas make us more thoughtful and more understanding when done correctly. But it has an uglier side, where outrage brews if a politician hasn't addressed an issue quickly enough, or where people dismiss a good representative because they don't quite "fit the mold."
* * *
### **The Amazon Prime Effect: Unrealistic Expectations**
In modern America, you can get almost anything custom-made and delivered to your door within days. Now with AI, you can even generate images of things that don't exist in seconds. This individualist drive at the heart of American capitalism also seeps into politics.
Every business should bend to your schedule. Every teacher should cater to your child uniquely. And every politician, it seems, should subscribe to your personal priorities and wants.
I'm all for holding Democrats to a high standard, and I've engaged in activism to move them on issues. There are innovative ways to run primaries against Democrats who have been abhorrent. There are fundamental tenets of the Party and of good governance that every candidate should meet. If they don't, they should be held accountable. That isn't the issue.
The problem comes when the bar is set so impossibly high that almost no one can clear it. When litmus tests are so bespoke that no one could keep them all straight, are we really engaging constructively--or just being too complicated?
* * *
### **The Moving Goalpost Game**
Politicians are human. They aren't the omniscient, omnipotent beings that media and history sometimes make them out to be. Expecting them to have all the answers sets up an impossible task: people demand responses on everything--even when it's irrelevant to their office.
Is it a requirement for a county board supervisor to have a stance on federal trade policy? No.
Does a school board member need a position on international conflicts overseas? Probably not. If they do, that's great--but what can they actually do about it?
Should a state delegate issue a statement on every national news story? Of course not, or they'd never stop posting.
Not everyone has to have a stance on every niche issue. On the campaign trail as a manager, I've heard it all:
* "Will your candidate divert traffic from my neighborhood so I don't hear as many cars?"
* "Where do they stand on letting citizens kill feral cats?"
* "Do they support removing astroturf fields from schools due to cancer risks?"
* "What about balloon pollution hurting coastal wildlife?"
* "What about bat populations and wind turbines?"

My answer was always: "I'll have to ask and get back to you." Because if I answered wrong, that's another lost vote. Politics has become a series of tests seemingly devised for candidates to fail.
And when the bar keeps moving, even politicians who change or grow face another hurdle: the question of whether it's ever "too late" to be right.
* * *
### **Too Late to Be Right?**
A common practice in activism is to pressure officials to improve on policy--whether online or in person. But too often, even when leaders come around, they're met with the dismissive response: "Well, it's too late now."
What's the point of persuading someone if you'll shame them for not having that view earlier? People learn. They grow. Shouldn't we applaud that?
We should give each other the grace of knowing that most people come to activism with good intentions. When people evolve, we should welcome them rather than punish them. Otherwise, we risk demotivating allies--or worse, fueling the petty, spiteful streaks that some politicians already carry.
* * *
### **There 's Only One AOC--and You Ain't Her**
Since 2018, we've had some great primary challengers who shook up the status quo. They've made our party and our democracy stronger. But more often, candidates run simply to run. They assume being younger, flashier, and good at branding is enough.
I've said it before: a candidate's viability isn't about their identity, their age, or their merch. It's about how they lead, how they treat people, what they stand for, and whether they show up with integrity.
Not every young candidate is AOC. Not every older incumbent is Joe Crowley, either. Some veterans in office are dead weight. Others are doing phenomenal work and don't deserve to be attacked for age alone.
When every incumbent faces a challenger--many without real cause--it's like the Boy Who Cried Wolf. People stop taking the challenges seriously, especially when the incumbent is both progressive and popular. Primaries are a powerful tool for progress, but not one to be wielded recklessly.
* * *
### **No One Size Fits All Candidate**
Monolithing all of our voters--and all of our candidates--is how we lose. Not every candidate will work in every locality, but pitting Democrats from very different districts against one another to decide "who is a better representation of the Party" is equally foolish.
For instance, Representative Abigail Spanberger and New York State Senator Zohran Mamdani may both run in the same year, but they are not running the same race. They serve different states, different communities, and vastly different constituencies. Mamdani's free rent proposal and Socialist label would never gain traction in most of Virginia, just as Spanberger's CIA and law enforcement background would not resonate in Mamdani's race.
And you know what? That's fine. That's the strength of the Democratic Party. We are a coalition--a spectrum of views and priorities--but we agree on far more than we disagree.
As I wrote in an earlier piece about Mamdani's race, he might not win a rural House seat in Virginia. But a Virginian candidate running a similarly bold, community-rooted, digital-forward, coalition-building campaign absolutely could. That's what we need to understand.
As long as our candidates are rooted in their districts and advancing an agenda that improves people's lives, why should one be deemed "more representative" of our Party than another? The measure of success shouldn't be conformity--it should be whether they deliver for the people they seek to represent.
* * *
### **Policy, Not Personality**
Our time is better spent advocating for policy than policing the "right kind of candidate." When we center politics on personalities, we lose sight of the fundamental goals. Policy is what endures.
Politics should be about the outcomes we create, not about worshipping personalities. Idolizing politicians prevents accountability--and blinds us to their humanity. The more we treat them as infallible, the more division and disappointment we invite.
Politicians are not deities. They're public servants. Our culture of fanaticism tricks us into thinking they're perfect beings, but they're just people who decided to do more.
Because at the end of the day, anyone can say the right things or present the right image. Authentic leadership is measured by what we deliver for our communities.
That means no politician is a perfect savior. We can support the individuals we believe will be the most effective advocates for our communities. If we get multiple good choices, even better--but at some point, we have to make a choice.
That's why I reject the idea that "both sides are the same," which drives people to stay home. But Democrats must show voters why we aren't the same. That means electing officials who champion policy that improves lives. And it means that post-primary, we champion our nominees.
What's happening right now in places like New York City and Minneapolis, where Democratic establishment figures refuse to support duly elected nominees who both happen to be Muslim, socialist State Senators.--shows the problem clearly. The "Blue No Matter Who" crowd quickly changes its tune when it's not their preferred candidate. If we want people to have faith in the Party, Democrats must practice what we preach: fight hard in primaries, but once voters have decided, help elect Democrats in November and hold them accountable afterward.
Because democracy doesn't work if support is conditional, if we want voters to believe in Democrats, Democrats have to believe in democracy--especially when the nominee isn't the one they wanted.
**TL;DR**
Voters often chase an impossible idea of the "perfect" candidate--expecting politicians to check every box, comment on every issue, and never make mistakes. This mindset creates unrealistic litmus tests that punish good representatives, discourage growth when politicians do evolve, and fuel unnecessary primary fights. Not every candidate is an AOC-style disruptor, and identity or branding alone doesn't make someone viable--integrity, policy, and service do.
At its core, politics should be about **policy, not personality**. Leaders are human, not saviors. If Democrats want to win and govern effectively, they need to hold candidates accountable while still uniting behind nominees post-primary. Otherwise, we risk eating ourselves alive while the other side advances unchecked.
Thank you for reading By the Ballot, a free substack that is supported 100% by you the subscribers. If you like what we are doing here, please remember to Like, Subscribe, and Share our work so we can continue to grow.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ieXRoZWJhbGxvdC5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWNjb3VudCIsInAiOjE3MTQxNzcxOCwicyI6NTE1NjU2NiwiZiI6dHJ1ZSwidSI6OTA1MDExOTIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzMzNDAyNSwiZXhwIjoyMDcyOTEwMDI1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMCIsInN1YiI6ImxpbmstcmVkaXJlY3QifQ.yl5pKfvwR2uamXIKEHbgQSnuO4itrKEJBWRjD1dYU8U?)
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/53ab6568-00bd-4c32-9759-2526275270cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

clean_body: We're chasing ghosts with impossible litmus tests | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? We're chasing ghosts with impossible litmus tests | | | Sep 8 | | | | | | | | | | | | Imagine your perfect political candidate. What's their background? Where do they come from? What do they look like? What is their belief system? Are they religious? Do they have a family? What's their sexual orientation? What's their gender identity? Where do they stand on current issues? National issues? International issues? Do you have them? Good. Chances are slim that any two readers will imagine the same candidate, given the infinite possible combinations. Unfortunately, this is also how many Americans approach the ballot box. Every individual voter is entitled to their own litmus test to determine whether or not they want to vote for someone. But when a candidate checks all but one of your boxes and you walk away, it creates an impossible environment where candidates must have an opinion on every niche issue--or risk losing votes or, worse, facing a digital dogpile for dismissing a potential voter. Living in a democracy is both a blessing and a curse when it comes to political discourse. The meeting and discussion of ideas make us more thoughtful and more understanding when done correctly. But it has an uglier side, where outrage brews if a politician hasn't addressed an issue quickly enough, or where people dismiss a good representative because they don't quite "fit the mold." The Amazon Prime Effect: Unrealistic Expectations In modern America, you can get almost anything custom-made and delivered to your door within days. Now with AI, you can even generate images of things that don't exist in seconds. This individualist drive at the heart of American capitalism also seeps into politics. Every business should bend to your schedule. Every teacher should cater to your child uniquely. And every politician, it seems, should subscribe to your personal priorities and wants. I'm all for holding Democrats to a high standard, and I've engaged in activism to move them on issues. There are innovative ways to run primaries against Democrats who have been abhorrent. There are fundamental tenets of the Party and of good governance that every candidate should meet. If they don't, they should be held accountable. That isn't the issue. The problem comes when the bar is set so impossibly high that almost no one can clear it. When litmus tests are so bespoke that no one could keep them all straight, are we really engaging constructively--or just being too complicated? The Moving Goalpost Game Politicians are human. They aren't the omniscient, omnipotent beings that media and history sometimes make them out to be. Expecting them to have all the answers sets up an impossible task: people demand responses on everything--even when it's irrelevant to their office. Is it a requirement for a county board supervisor to have a stance on federal trade policy? No. Does a school board member need a position on international conflicts overseas? Probably not. If they do, that's great--but what can they actually do about it? Should a state delegate issue a statement on every national news story? Of course not, or they'd never stop posting. Not everyone has to have a stance on every niche issue. On the campaign trail as a manager, I've heard it all: "Will your candidate divert traffic from my neighborhood so I don't hear as many cars?" "Where do they stand on letting citizens kill feral cats?" "Do they support removing astroturf fields from schools due to cancer risks?" "What about balloon pollution hurting coastal wildlife?" "What about bat populations and wind turbines?" My answer was always: "I'll have to ask and get back to you." Because if I answered wrong, that's another lost vote. Politics has become a series of tests seemingly devised for candidates to fail. And when the bar keeps moving, even politicians who change or grow face another hurdle: the question of whether it's ever "too late" to be right. Too Late to Be Right? A common practice in activism is to pressure officials to improve on policy--whether online or in person. But too often, even when leaders come around, they're met with the dismissive response: "Well, it's too late now." What's the point of persuading someone if you'll shame them for not having that view earlier? People learn. They grow. Shouldn't we applaud that? We should give each other the grace of knowing that most people come to activism with good intentions. When people evolve, we should welcome them rather than punish them. Otherwise, we risk demotivating allies--or worse, fueling the petty, spiteful streaks that some politicians already carry. There 's Only One AOC--and You Ain't Her Since 2018, we've had some great primary challengers who shook up the status quo. They've made our party and our democracy stronger. But more often, candidates run simply to run. They assume being younger, flashier, and good at branding is enough. I've said it before: a candidate's viability isn't about their identity, their age, or their merch. It's about how they lead, how they treat people, what they stand for, and whether they show up with integrity. Not every young candidate is AOC. Not every older incumbent is Joe Crowley, either. Some veterans in office are dead weight. Others are doing phenomenal work and don't deserve to be attacked for age alone. When every incumbent faces a challenger--many without real cause--it's like the Boy Who Cried Wolf. People stop taking the challenges seriously, especially when the incumbent is both progressive and popular. Primaries are a powerful tool for progress, but not one to be wielded recklessly. No One Size Fits All Candidate Monolithing all of our voters--and all of our candidates--is how we lose. Not every candidate will work in every locality, but pitting Democrats from very different districts against one another to decide "who is a better representation of the Party" is equally foolish. For instance, Representative Abigail Spanberger and New York State Senator Zohran Mamdani may both run in the same year, but they are not running the same race. They serve different states, different communities, and vastly different constituencies. Mamdani's free rent proposal and Socialist label would never gain traction in most of Virginia, just as Spanberger's CIA and law enforcement background would not resonate in Mamdani's race. And you know what? That's fine. That's the strength of the Democratic Party. We are a coalition--a spectrum of views and priorities--but we agree on far more than we disagree. As I wrote in an earlier piece about Mamdani's race, he might not win a rural House seat in Virginia. But a Virginian candidate running a similarly bold, community-rooted, digital-forward, coalition-building campaign absolutely could. That's what we need to understand. As long as our candidates are rooted in their districts and advancing an agenda that improves people's lives, why should one be deemed "more representative" of our Party than another? The measure of success shouldn't be conformity--it should be whether they deliver for the people they seek to represent. Policy, Not Personality Our time is better spent advocating for policy than policing the "right kind of candidate." When we center politics on personalities, we lose sight of the fundamental goals. Policy is what endures. Politics should be about the outcomes we create, not about worshipping personalities. Idolizing politicians prevents accountability--and blinds us to their humanity. The more we treat them as infallible, the more division and disappointment we invite. Politicians are not deities. They're public servants. Our culture of fanaticism tricks us into thinking they're perfect beings, but they're just people who decided to do more. Because at the end of the day, anyone can say the right things or present the right image. Authentic leadership is measured by what we deliver for our communities. That means no politician is a perfect savior. We can support the individuals we believe will be the most effective advocates for our communities. If we get multiple good choices, even better--but at some point, we have to make a choice. That's why I reject the idea that "both sides are the same," which drives people to stay home. But Democrats must show voters why we aren't the same. That means electing officials who champion policy that improves lives. And it means that post-primary, we champion our nominees. What's happening right now in places like New York City and Minneapolis, where Democratic establishment figures refuse to support duly elected nominees who both happen to be Muslim, socialist State Senators.--shows the problem clearly. The "Blue No Matter Who" crowd quickly changes its tune when it's not their preferred candidate. If we want people to have faith in the Party, Democrats must practice what we preach: fight hard in primaries, but once voters have decided, help elect Democrats in November and hold them accountable afterward. Because democracy doesn't work if support is conditional, if we want voters to believe in Democrats, Democrats have to believe in democracy--especially when the nominee isn't the one they wanted. TL;DR Voters often chase an impossible idea of the "perfect" candidate--expecting politicians to check every box, comment on every issue, and never make mistakes. This mindset creates unrealistic litmus tests that punish good representatives, discourage growth when politicians do evolve, and fuel unnecessary primary fights. Not every candidate is an AOC-style disruptor, and identity or branding alone doesn't make someone viable--integrity, policy, and service do. At its core, politics should be about policy, not personality. Leaders are human, not saviors. If Democrats want to win and govern effectively, they need to hold candidates accountable while still uniting behind nominees post-primary. Otherwise, we risk eating ourselves alive while the other side advances unchecked. Thank you for reading By the Ballot, a free substack that is supported 100% by you the subscribers. If you like what we are doing here, please remember to Like, Subscribe, and Share our work so we can continue to grow. ? | | ---|---|--- By the Ballot is an opinion series published on Substack. All views expressed are solely those of the author and should not be interpreted as reporting or objective journalism or attributed to any other individual or organization. I am not a journalist or reporter, nor do I claim to be one. This publication represents personal commentary, analysis, and opinion only. What is By the Ballot?** By the Ballot is a free SubStack that features unapologetic dispatches from the New South 's next generation of Democrats. Commentary on stances, tactics, and news on the Left. We don't claim to know everything or always be right, we just want to be a part of the conversation. While there 's many different ways to power and the Right wants to take over our country by hook or by crook, we want to help Democrats help Americans by making a government for the people by the ballot. Make sure to follow us on socials to stay up to date on our work! Twitter- Instagram- BlueSky - Contact us at substackbytheballot@gmail.com | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Matt Royer Herndon, VA 20171 ? ?

name: Jessica Hamilton, Evergreen
subject: Join us for Sun Day ☀️
body:
Be a part of the clean energy revolution
| | | | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3953650/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| Hi there,

We’re winning the clean energy fight—but we need to win it _faster_.

Solar installations are breaking records every month. Electric vehicles are outselling gas cars in key markets. Clean energy jobs are growing faster than any other sector. The transition we’ve been working toward for years is finally happening at scale.

**But powerful fossil fuel interests and their allies in government are fighting back harder than ever, spending millions to slow progress and protect their profits.**

They’re rolling back clean energy policies, spreading misinformation, and blocking the infrastructure we need to accelerate this transition.

**That’s why Evergreen is joining**[**Sun Day**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3953651/87SdQ4wZ)**on September 21** —a nationwide celebration of clean energy progress and a call to accelerate the transition that’s already underway. We’ll be joining communities across the country to showcase the businesses, organizations, and families that are leading the way to a clean energy future.

[**Join us for Sun Day on September 21 by signing up to host or attend a Sun Day event!**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3953652/87SdQ4wZ) |
| [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3953653/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| There are lots of ways to get involved:
* Host or attend a solar open house in your community
* Join a clean energy parade or rally
* Go for an EV test drive
* Share your solar savings story
* Participate in digital actions
* Connect with local clean energy businesses

|
---
| The clean energy revolution is happening. We need to make sure it happens fast enough and that everyone can benefit from it.

Whether you're motivated by saving money on your energy bills, breathing cleaner air, fighting climate change, or supporting good-paying American jobs—**Sun Day is for you.**

[**I hope you’ll join us by finding—or organizing!—an event near you and registering at sunday.earth.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3953654/87SdQ4wZ) |
---
| See you on September 21, |
---
| | |
---
### Jessica Hamilton
Senior Director of Digital Advocacy
Evergreen Collaborative
---
---
* * *
---
| _Evergreen is building the ambitious, actionable policy roadmap for an all-out mobilization to defeat climate change—and to create millions of jobs in a thriving, just, and inclusive clean energy future. None of this would be possible without your support._ |
---
[DONATE](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3953655/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
---
| | [ ](https://evergreenaction.com/?utm_source=digital_template&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=evergreen&utm_content=footer)

name: With Honor Action
subject: 🚨 Announcing a New With Honor x Gallup Research Partnership 🚨
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/2207420a3031/bats-brews-and-a-birthday-salute-to-the-us-army-14054012?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | |
---
| | | | Friends,

Last week, our partners at the With Honor Institute announced a new study with Gallup to explore how Americans view veterans and their role in public life. The survey will ask whether people connect qualities like leadership, integrity, and bridge-building to military service—and whether those traits inspire trust in veterans as political leaders.

By adding veteran-focused questions to Gallup’s long-running national surveys, this partnership will offer fresh insights into how veterans are seen across civic, business, and political life. The first results arrive this fall, and we hope they spark meaningful conversations about the unique role veterans can play in uniting our country.

📝 You can read the Institute's full press release [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=929df602b5&e=33f53a5b1a).
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=0a5c755258&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
|
---
|
---
| Right before Labor Day, communities across the country received great news: the White House agreed to release more than $184 million in AmeriCorps funding that had been on hold. Thanks to thousands of emails, calls, and meetings with state leaders and lawmakers, this critical funding for national service is fully restored. This means AmeriCorps members can return to classrooms, disaster sites, and community centers, bringing much-needed help and hope to the places that need it most.

But the fight is not over.

Last Tuesday, the House of Representatives advanced a bill that would slash over 50 percent of AmeriCorps' funding. This cut would devastate communities, once again putting AmeriCorps' future into doubt and undercutting its ability to serve and support programs that rebuild homes and save lives.

The fight for AmeriCorps remains bipartisan. Earlier this year, Reps. Don Bacon and Jimmy Panetta, veterans and founding Co-Chairs of the For Country Caucus, joined With Honor to discuss the importance of maintaining and expanding opportunities for Americans to serve. You can watch a clip from that talk [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=553917c94c&e=33f53a5b1a).

With Honor Action continues to work with our partners at Voices for National Service, and we urge you to make your voices heard and take action to protect national service in America.
---
| [Contact Your Lawmaker](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=6651637dd4&e=33f53a5b1a "Contact Your Lawmaker")
---
|
---
| "It's the highest-ranking paratrooper that's supposed to jump first. So you take the risks first, you suffer the most, and then you eat last. That's servant leadership." — Rep. Jason Crow, former Co-Chair of the For Country Caucus
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=c34abc078f&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| Rep. Jason Crow recently joined American Conversations with Heather Cox Richardson, reflecting on how his service as an Army Ranger in Iraq and Afghanistan shapes his approach to leadership today. He spoke about the values of "servant leadership"—putting others first, building trust, and bridging divides—that continue to guide his work in Congress today. As part of his commitment to working across the aisle, Rep. Crow spoke about his work with the For Country Caucus, where members from both parties come together as veterans to support one another, find common ground, and put service to country above politics.

📺 You can watch the full episode [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=0909ef1115&e=33f53a5b1a).
---
|
---
| | | | Please follow With Honor Action on Twitter via this [link](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=4858a24e0d&e=33f53a5b1a),
and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country.
---
| | | | | | [](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=c317a330cd&e=33f53a5b1a)
---

---
| | [](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=edc413a88e&e=33f53a5b1a)

Thanks to thousands of emails, calls, and meetings with state leaders and lawmakers, this critical funding for national service is fully restored. This means AmeriCorps members can return to classrooms, disaster sites, and community centers, bringing much-needed help and hope to the places that need it most. But the fight is not over. Last Tuesday, the House of Representatives advanced a bill that would slash over 50 percent of AmeriCorps' funding. This cut would devastate communities, once again putting AmeriCorps' future into doubt and undercutting its ability to serve and support programs that rebuild homes and save lives. The fight for AmeriCorps remains bipartisan. Earlier this year, Reps. Don Bacon and Jimmy Panetta, veterans and founding Co-Chairs of the For Country Caucus, joined With Honor to discuss the importance of maintaining and expanding opportunities for Americans to serve. You can watch a clip from that talk . With Honor Action continues to work with our partners at Voices for National Service, and we urge you to make your voices heard and take action to protect national service in America. | "Contact Your Lawmaker" | | "It's the highest-ranking paratrooper that's supposed to jump first. So you take the risks first, you suffer the most, and then you eat last. That's servant leadership." — Rep. Jason Crow, former Co-Chair of the For Country Caucus | | Rep. Jason Crow recently joined American Conversations with Heather Cox Richardson, reflecting on how his service as an Army Ranger in Iraq and Afghanistan shapes his approach to leadership today. He spoke about the values of "servant leadership"—putting others first, building trust, and bridging divides—that continue to guide his work in Congress today. As part of his commitment to working across the aisle, Rep. Crow spoke about his work with the For Country Caucus, where members from both parties come together as veterans to support one another, find common ground, and put service to country above politics. 📺 You can watch the full episode . | | | | | Please follow With Honor Action on Twitter via this , and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country. | | | | | | | | | | | | PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC | | With Honor Action is a 501(c)(4), which serves as the organization’s policy and social-welfare arm. With Honor has an affiliated federally registered “super PAC” called With Honor Fund. | © 2025 With Honor. All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: 700 Pennsylvania Avenue, SE Washington D.C. 20003 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or .

body:
We need to act now >>

| Hi friend, we’re reaching out again because the threat to the U.S. Postal Service hasn’t gone away — in fact, it’s getting worse.

Donald Trump is doubling down on his push to privatize the USPS, **even though it’s protected by the Constitution and essential to millions of Americans.**

If he succeeds in handing over a vital public service to private corporations, it would set a dangerous precedent that presidents can dismantle core public services without any oversight.

New Dems are fighting back, but we need the backing of the American people who are paying attention and ready to push back.

**[Will you take a moment to add your name and stand with us to protect the Postal Service? »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114816375/568248334/830179060?refcode=NDCAF_20250908_EMPET_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZjI2MmRiOS1iMzhjLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=74ZMDtq3Kf2OajjXaZtuV2VMCtcKwst8iN9s_CYoSBY=&emci=fe903cbd-858a-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=0f262db9-b38c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=48524772)** | [YES, I’M STANDING WITH NEW DEMS TO DEFEND THE USPS! »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114816378/568248336/830179060?refcode=NDCAF_20250908_EMPET_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZjI2MmRiOS1iMzhjLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=74ZMDtq3Kf2OajjXaZtuV2VMCtcKwst8iN9s_CYoSBY=&emci=fe903cbd-858a-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=0f262db9-b38c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=48524772)

Here's what you need to know and what you can do to help save the Postal Service.

1\. **The Post Office is enshrined in the US Constitution and created by an act of Congress.** _Trump’s efforts to unilaterally dismantle it are unconstitutional!_

2\. **Privatizing USPS would mean higher costs, slower delivery, and the loss of universal service** — especially for rural communities, seniors, and people with disabilities.

3\. **It’s a threat to our elections.** Millions depend on the USPS to vote by mail. Weakening it makes it harder for everyday Americans to participate in our democracy.

We can’t allow Trump to dismantle the USPS and privatize it completely.

**[As New Dems fight to defend working Americans’ access to the USPS, we need you to have our back! Can you add your name to defend the USPS? »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114816387/568248343/830179060?refcode=NDCAF_20250908_EMPET_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZjI2MmRiOS1iMzhjLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=74ZMDtq3Kf2OajjXaZtuV2VMCtcKwst8iN9s_CYoSBY=&emci=fe903cbd-858a-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=0f262db9-b38c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=48524772)**

subject: Trump wants to be America’s dictator.

body:
From troops in American streets to gerrymandered districts—this is the fight of our lifetime...for OUR republic.
| | | | Can't see this email? [View in Your Browser](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K4MNZRAPRNJH6D7PV0Q9JX98_0)
---
| | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K4MNZRAPRNJH6D7PV0Q9JX98_1)
---
| | | | Dear Peter, Donald Trump is not hiding it anymore: he wants to be America’s dictator. After firing independent officials, silencing institutions, and making powerful law firms bend a knee, Trump’s every move is clearly designed to consolidate power for his own benefit. He doesn’t care about you or me, or even his Republican Party; he only cares about himself. Now, Trump is working to rig the 2026 elections (if he actually allows them) through extreme gerrymandering—drawing maps that silence voters, especially in minority and working-class communities. This isn’t democracy; it’s unconstitutional Trumpism. Sadly, it doesn’t stop there. Trump is even turning America’s economy into his personal fiefdom. His meddling in the business of tech giants like Nvidia and Intel. He’s normalizing militarization of cities, jockeying the economy to profit from public office, demanding government stakes in private companies (ahem...socialism), rewarding favorites, and punishing critics to silence dissent. That’s not free enterprise. That’s dictatorship economics. Some have pointed out that it took Vladimir Putin a decade after taking office to do what Trump has accomplished in seven months. Our nation was founded on the idea of standing up to autocrats and tyranny. Every generation has had to answer the question: _Will we defend our democracy against those who seek to destroy it?_ Today, that duty falls on us. We need you in this fight. Every dollar is a stand against authoritarianism. Every contribution is a voice for democracy. **Chip in right now** to help us defend our elections, protect our freedoms, and ensure that Trump’s march towards a throne ends here.
---
| | [ Donate Now ](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K4MNZRAPRNJH6D7PV0Q9JX98_2)
---
| This is bigger than one election. It’s about the future of America. It’s about our children. Stand with us. Stand for democracy. — Tennessee House Democratic Caucus

---
| | | |
---
| _Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_

name: Kendall Qualls
subject: Minnesota could flip red
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Kendall Qualls for Governor Committee | PO Box 235, Stillwater, MN 55082
body:
Here’s how we make it happen

| | | | [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zbnbs0seru7o1c9pimd6gcglfup5vac3p5e35ceo_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
---
| | Friend,
I’m Kendall Qualls, a Conservative outsider running for governor of Minnesota to defeat far-left Democrat Tim Walz and stop his dangerous, radical agenda.
**[Before you read any further, I have to ask,**_will you chip in $25, $50, or anything you can today to help me win this critical race?_**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z1i64q6oq98msrcot9iqqvt8pdvsc7v7kj3ac2lo_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)**

---
[CHIP IN NOW →](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zusr8itkff55d0s46ln5b8i4m6nh5a7fssocggro_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
| | Walz’s record is a warning for America: **higher taxes, bigger government, weaker public safety, and divisive social policies.**
If he wins again, Democrats will point to Minnesota as proof their radical playbook works, and copy it in other states.
**I know what real opportunity looks like because I’ve lived it.** From growing up in poverty to serving as a U.S. Army officer to leading in the private sector, I’ve never been part of the political establishment. _I’m ready to take it on._
This race isn’t just about Minnesota. **It’s about proving Conservative leadership can still win, even in a blue state.**
But to do that, I need a national wave of support right now.
**If Conservatives across America rally behind this campaign, we can shock the political class, flip Minnesota red, and send a message the left will never forget.**
**_[Will you chip in a donation to support our campaign today?](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zeencorshjce38agv9dj7s116qsn6tn6tuckkg20_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)_**

---
[DONATE $25](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zcfk9l79jfvbdv2vbuktmq5lbmppma64c0mtn8ho_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
[DONATE $50](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zte54leudq0ki9erqeglfkmumcl7pk8mqj6t1qg0_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
[DONATE $100](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zku89oooil1k1c4sdug874ich4f4ns9frvqqmhh8_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
[DONATE ANY AMOUNT](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z6bud8585dio4mtep1l2aegj5fkou5onfn2vr90o_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
| | Thank you for standing with me in this fight.
Sincerely, Kendall Qualls
Candidate for Governor of Minnesota

---
|
---
| | [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zv9s8mbkprk8rp2qkg464v5gk5amqlbo0gh0pn38_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
---

My name is Kendall Qualls. I wasn’t born into privilege or power. I was raised in Harlem, New York, in the 1960s; a child of divorce and one of several siblings living in public housing. Before I reached middle school, I moved to a trailer park in Oklahoma to live with my father. Neither of my parents finished high school.
I didn’t take shortcuts. I worked full-time to put myself through college. I used student loans and paid them back in full. I served as an officer in the United States Army. I earned an MBA from the University of Michigan. Then, I spent 30 years leading teams and driving innovation in the healthcare industry — rising to become Global Vice President of an $850 million business unit.
Now, I’m running for Governor of Minnesota to make sure that the principles that lifted me - hard work, faith, family, personal responsibility, and the free enterprise system - are preserved, defended, and passed on to the next generation.
We don’t need more government. We need more opportunities.
That’s what I’m fighting for, and I need your help. [Can I count on your support today?](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zimmhdpejfmb9600u76egl99olj9b8f5aq3bhi98_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
With gratitude,Kendall Qualls

[DONATE](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zb6vrngljjg3mpsl2im25pck2is6892tkn304i4g_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)
|

---
| | **The fight to take back Minnesota is on, and every day counts.**
_[Shop the official Kendall Qualls Store today and wear your support with pride.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zl1imkb4fsv12g2mt07jku8ier3jvmvumvm14pv0_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)_
When you get your gear, you’re making a statement that goes far beyond what you wear. It’s a message to Tim Walz that Minnesotans are ready for new leadership, safer communities, and a restored American Dream. Your support helps fuel Kendall’s campaign and spreads his vision of conservative leadership everywhere you go.
Join the movement. Show your pride. Let’s win this together.

---
[SHOP THE STORE](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zh1e1504junqnb38n9rl8uglebfau0pdh2vi67n8_rp22sh2s8i6epb7cpim0o74cth2bs)

name: Sen. Jacky Rosen
subject: Response requested from dpwillis67@gmail.com
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
I need to know your top priorities now that the Senate is back in session.
| | |
---
Friend,
The Senate is just getting back in session, but I haven’t stopped fighting Donald Trump and the GOP’s disastrous agenda. Here’s what I’ve been working on:
1) **Repealing cuts to Medicaid.** On the 60th anniversary of Medicaid, I helped introduce legislation to repeal the GOP’s massive cuts to Medicaid that could cause millions of Americans to lose their health care. The bill also includes a permanent extension of the Affordable Care Act’s (ACA) enhanced premium tax credits.
2) **Cutting housing costs.** I introduced a bill to help lower housing costs for Nevada families. The Housing Oversight and Mitigating Exploitation (HOME) Act would crack down on price gouging by corporate investors who are buying up homes and driving up prices.
3) **Fighting for food assistance.** I joined Catholic Charities to hand deliver Meals on Wheels and highlight the critical importance of similar food assistance programs. I also vowed to do everything I can to restore SNAP funding and reauthorize the Older Americans Act to protect the Meals on Wheels program.
I know there's no shortage of concerns weighing on your mind. So now that the Senate is back in session, I want to know what fights and issues are most important to you right now. **That’s why I've launched a new survey, and I’m looking for 75 more responses from 37087 before midnight. ****[Will you please take just two minutes to complete my priorities survey now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114817601/568252585/-979897890?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=37087&ask=5&amounts=250,100,50,25,5&code=jtk5601-su-nd&refcode=jtk5601-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZDJhMjU2Yi1iNDhjLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R5mlOs7R6JfPeAbXDRuS_R0KeV9ujdS9TnIUU2cw1EY=&emci=834e6517-fc88-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=ad2a256b-b48c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=3800590)**
| | [Take My Survey >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114817602/568252586/-979897890?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=37087&ask=5&amounts=250,100,50,25,5&code=jtk5601-su-nd&refcode=jtk5601-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZDJhMjU2Yi1iNDhjLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R5mlOs7R6JfPeAbXDRuS_R0KeV9ujdS9TnIUU2cw1EY=&emci=834e6517-fc88-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=ad2a256b-b48c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=3800590)

Thank you,

Jacky
|

Jacky Rosen:**

Friend, I’m fighting tirelessly in the Senate to deliver progress for people across Nevada and our country. But with Donald Trump in the White House and Republicans in control of both houses of Congress, I need your grassroots support to keep up the fights that matter most.

Senate Democrats like me are the only thing standing in the way of Trump's extreme agenda that will hurt everyday Americans. That's why I need people like you on my side to help defend our progress and take back power from the GOP.

**[Will you chip in now to turn Nevada blue and take back the Senate majority?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114817604/568252588/616501713?refcode=footer&code=jtk5601-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZDJhMjU2Yi1iNDhjLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R5mlOs7R6JfPeAbXDRuS_R0KeV9ujdS9TnIUU2cw1EY=)** You can also **[text JACKY to 30508](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114817605/568252589/616501713?refcode=footer&code=jtk5601-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZDJhMjU2Yi1iNDhjLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R5mlOs7R6JfPeAbXDRuS_R0KeV9ujdS9TnIUU2cw1EY=)** to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive updates from my campaign.

Thank you,
Jacky _By entering your mobile number, you agree to receive periodic text messages from Rosen for Nevada. Message and data rates may apply. Text STOP to quit or HELP for help.[Privacy policy.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114817606/568252590/1198389084?code=jtk5601-su-nd&refcode=jtk5601-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZDJhMjU2Yi1iNDhjLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=R5mlOs7R6JfPeAbXDRuS_R0KeV9ujdS9TnIUU2cw1EY=&emci=834e6517-fc88-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=ad2a256b-b48c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=3800590)_

Virginia Republican gubernatorial candidate Winsome Sears **[put Jake Tapper in his place](https://substack.com/redirect/86477944-1f48-43a2-83fb-88acc3b6e616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during a tense interview on CNN after he belittled her effort to combat woke gender ideology in Virginia's schools.
"Is this issue of trans rights the issue that most Virginians are concerned about?" Tapper asked Sears doubtfully.
"My opponent has voted against parents' rights, against parents knowing what is really happening in schools!" Sears fired back.
**[Watch the live clash here:](https://substack.com/redirect/86477944-1f48-43a2-83fb-88acc3b6e616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

# Footage shows final moments before psychotic thug stabs 23-year-old Ukrainian refugee on Charlotte train
Surveillance footage from Charlotte's light rail system shows 23-year-old Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska being fatally **[stabbed by a stranger](https://substack.com/redirect/3e314796-a15b-49c4-a5c2-1a2a4ef76696?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on August 22, with the video revealing the unprovoked attack occurred around four minutes after she boarded the train in her pizzeria uniform.
## Watch the horrific moment:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/28753fa5-9396-4c0a-b1bf-c53bbd41b4f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Random Violence:** The suspect, 34-year-old Decarlos Brown, allegedly stabbed Zarutska three times including at least once in the neck before calmly walking through the train car and waiting by the doors as passengers witnessed the attack; police confirmed the victim and attacker did not know each other.
* **Criminal History:** Brown has an extensive criminal record spanning over a decade, including previous arrests for felony larceny, robbery with a dangerous weapon, and communicating threats, before being charged with first-degree murder in Zarutska's death.
* **Public Safety Concerns:** The killing has intensified debates about transit safety in Charlotte, with city council members expressing concern about violence on public transportation and Republican Rep. Brenden Jones blaming the incident on what he characterized as "woke agendas" prioritizing criminals over public safety.

* * *
# Rand Paul picks a fight with JD Vance over cartel killing remark
Sen. Rand Paul **[sharply criticized Vice President JD Vance](https://substack.com/redirect/a029a0e6-51fd-4238-929a-bbeaf297a65a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** on Saturday after Vance declared that killing cartel members "is the highest and best use of our military," with the Kentucky Republican calling it "despicable" to glorify executions without trials.
* **Vance's Statement:** Vice President JD Vance declared on X that "killing cartel members who poison our fellow citizens is the highest and best use of our military," responding to President Trump's video showing what he called a "kinetic strike" against Tren de Aragua members transporting drugs in international waters.
* **Paul's Objection:** Republican Sen. Rand Paul strongly criticized Vance's position, calling it "despicable and thoughtless" to glorify killing people without trial or legal representation, referencing "To Kill a Mockingbird" and questioning what happens when accused individuals are executed without due process.
* **War Crime Concerns:** When someone suggested that killing foreign civilians without due process constitutes a war crime, Vance responded defiantly with "I don't give a s--- what you call it," showing no concern for legal or procedural considerations.
* **Republican Division:** The exchange revealed a split among Republicans, with Sen. Bernie Moreno of Ohio defending Vance by arguing that protecting American lives from "foreign terrorist drug traffickers" justifies the approach, while Paul maintained his constitutional concerns about extrajudicial killings.

* * *
# Trump gives Hamas "last warning" to release all remaining hostages - "There will not be another one!"
President Trump **[issued what he called his "last warning"](https://substack.com/redirect/db26192b-763d-4d15-ac3a-d92c6e7a5894?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to Hamas on Truth Social, demanding the terror group release the remaining hostages or face unspecified "consequences," stating that Israel has accepted his terms and it's time for Hamas to do the same.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8b7db244-81eb-4662-9822-ab5b84521678?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Trump wrote:**
> _" Everyone wants the Hostages HOME. Everyone wants this War to end! The Israelis have accepted my Terms. It is time for Hamas to accept as well. I have warned Hamas about the consequences of not accepting. This is my last warning, there will not be another one!"_
* **Hostage Status:** Trump indicated that roughly 20 hostages are still believed to be alive in Gaza, though he suggested some may have recently died, while acknowledging that at least 30 hostages are confirmed dead and negotiations continue for their return.
* **Negotiation Challenges:** The president warned that securing the release of the final hostages would be difficult and likely require "capitulation," suggesting that extracting the last 10-20 captives would demand significant concessions.
* **Escalating Tensions:** The ultimatum comes as Israel has intensified military operations in Gaza, with the IDF warning Palestinian civilians to evacuate ahead of planned airstrikes, and follows Hamas's release of disturbing footage showing two hostages taken during the October 7, 2023 attack.

name: Jose Javier Rodriguez
subject: I'm not your typical politician.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSE JAVIER RODRIGUEZ FOR FLORIDA ATTORNEY GENERAL
body:
Here's my story: | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114820118/568260295/1546157161?refcode=em_JJR_FR_biofocused_090825&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDkxMjYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmExMzJhZDEtYjU4Yy1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=TPODJxAK6xQTwLap7CXqca5Fu1K3TnGpHBPl9Sto15c=&emci=744f221c-cf89-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=fa132ad1-b58c-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=32374386)
---
Peter, When I was growing up, my father would tell me stories about leaving Cuba as a teenager through Operation Pedro Pan. He left a dictatorship that crushed the hopes of ordinary people and brought his faith and determination to America. My upbringing was centered on community and looking out for others. I was taught a simple lesson: when you see a bully picking on someone, you stand up and fight back, no matter how powerful they are. That lesson has guided my life and much of my career. I've dedicated my entire career to fighting for the voiceless as a workers' rights attorney, state senator, and Assistant Secretary of Labor. I've taken on wage thieves who steal from janitors and construction workers. I've battled utility giants trying to rig the system. I've stood up to corrupt politicians who put profit over people. But my fight is only beginning. **[Florida needs more leaders who aren't afraid to take on the powerful interests making life too expensive and less safe for working families. With your support, we can build a movement that takes back the Attorney General's office for the people. Will you chip in any amount you can afford right now to help me become the People's Lawyer Florida desperately needs?>>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114820121/568260296/1963674758?refcode=em_JJR_FR_biofocused_090825&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDkxMjYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmExMzJhZDEtYjU4Yy1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=TPODJxAK6xQTwLap7CXqca5Fu1K3TnGpHBPl9Sto15c=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | **[DONATE $10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114820123/568260298/1963674758?amount=10&express_lane=true&refcode=em_JJR_FR_biofocused_090825&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDkxMjYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmExMzJhZDEtYjU4Yy1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=TPODJxAK6xQTwLap7CXqca5Fu1K3TnGpHBPl9Sto15c=) **
